course_title: Design Thinking for Human-Centred Value Creation

learning_outcomes.description: Analyse complex problems by applying theoretical and practical knowledge of design thinking, creativity, and innovation.
Generate creative ideas and solutions to improve current practice using a range of design thinking models and techniques.
Persuade an audience to support design thinking processes and recommendations.
Collaboratively validate potential solutions for customer satisfaction and well-being.
Engage authentically and inclusively with diverse community stakeholders.

assessments.assessment_title: Building Empathy Through User Journey Mapping
assessments.task_description:
In this individual assignment you will develop a report on the project "Building Empathy Through User Journey Mapping".

You will:

  • select one of a series of given scenarios, analyse it, and build empathy with end-users in such scenario through interviews, and
  • report on your work in a report containing, among others, a detailed journey map of the end-users' experience in the scenario, and
  • use a range of creativity and design thinking techniques in your work.

AI Statement

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit online through Turnitin located in the Blackboard Assessment link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Presentation Slides 25/05/2026 3:00 pm

Presentation During Class Week 13,

Presentation Slides are due before class begins on Monday. Presentation is During Class.
assessments.task_description:
In this group assignment students present their Ideation, Prototyping, and Testing project in a live presentation.

Live as well as off-line feedback will be offered to help students with refining their work in preparation for the final, individual reflection (see Assessment 3).

Students submit the presentation slides via Blackboard.

For group assessments, a peer evaluation is required, the results of which may influence final individual grades.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.

AI Statement:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit presentation slides via Blackboard before class begins, by the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Individual Reflection via Real-time Online Interview
assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires students to complete an individual, real-time interview-style video reflection via Bongo, critically reflecting on their learning and industry feedback, with formative practice questions provided in advance.
